rationale_for_baseline_tier: A high-probability pulmonary embolism with markers of haemodynamic compromise (presyncope, hypoxaemia at 92%, resting tachycardia/tachypnoea) and clinician-detected right heart strain represents an immediate threat to life that can deteriorate to obstructive shock and cardiac arrest within a short time. This mandates T5 — call 000 for an emergency ambulance to a hospital able to perform CT pulmonary angiography and deliver anticoagulation/thrombolysis. The patient must be kept still to avoid dislodging further clot.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Worsening faintness, collapse, or loss of consciousness
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — stay on the line with 000
escalation_edges.rationale: Collapse signals progression to obstructive shock or cardiac arrest from a massive embolus; paramedic resuscitation is the critical resource.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If she becomes very faint or collapses, call 000 back straight away, lay her flat, and if she stops breathing normally begin CPR — the operator will talk you through it.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Any urge or attempt to walk, stand or rub the swollen calf
escalation_edges.rationale: Movement or massage of the affected calf can dislodge further thrombus and cause a larger, potentially fatal embolus.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Please do not stand up, walk, or rub your sore leg for any reason — keep completely still and sitting upright until the ambulance arrives.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Worsening breathlessness, rising chest pain, blue lips, or a falling oxygen reading
escalation_edges.rationale: Deteriorating hypoxaemia or breathlessness indicates enlarging embolic obstruction and impending respiratory failure.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your breathing gets worse, your lips look blue, or your oxygen reading drops, tell the 000 operator immediately — help is on the way.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Spouse is present and able to help monitor and, if needed, perform bystander CPR under 000 guidance
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Engage the spouse: keep the patient still and upright, unlock the front door for paramedics, watch for collapse, and be ready to follow 000 CPR instructions if breathing stops.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 now for an emergency ambulance and say you are worried about a possible blood clot in the lung with chest pain, breathlessness and near-fainting. Keep the patient sitting upright and completely still — do not let her walk, stand or rub the sore calf. Take slow, shallow breaths to ease the pain. Stay with her and unlock the door for the paramedics.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Call 000 immediately for a Priority 1 ambulance
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours a day — this is a life-threatening emergency and must not wait for clinic hours or a GP appointment.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide a discharge summary with the CTPA result, anticoagulation plan and planned duration; take these to the GP for ongoing VTE follow-up and risk-factor review.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating the presentation as non-urgent (self-care, routine or same-day GP) — e.g. labelling it musculoskeletal chest pain or anxiety and reassuring home.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An untreated submassive/massive embolus progresses to right ventricular failure and sudden cardiac arrest within hours; the patient dies at home.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Recognising urgency but advising self-transport to the emergency department rather than an ambulance — sudden collapse during self-transport with no resuscitation capability is the harm; a monitored ambulance is required for a haemodynamically unstable PE.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Over-triage is not a meaningful risk here — T5 is correct; calling 000 for this presentation is appropriate even if the clot ultimately proves small, and under-triage is far more dangerous.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Telehealth cannot confirm PE (no CTPA) or measure true blood pressure and right heart strain, so the exact severity is uncertain.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat a high-probability, potentially life-threatening PE as an emergency and escalate to 000 — act on the pattern, do not wait for confirmatory tests that can only be done in hospital.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Using the inability to confirm the diagnosis remotely as a reason to downgrade urgency, seek a D-dimer first, or reassure the patient home.
